<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://ma.tt/2015/03/pink-and-blue/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>Speaking of color masculinity</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, here’s Kanye on creativity, society, and color from</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.thefader.com/2012/11/29/kanye-west-im-amazing%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>his 2008 FADER interview</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">I feel like all the words are in you, you’re just blocking yourself, you’re blocking your creativity. Society has put up so many boundaries, so many limitations on what’s right and wrong that it’s almost impossible to get a pure thought out. It’s like a little kid, a little boy, looking at colors, and no one told him what colors are good, before somebody tells you you shouldn’t like pink because that’s for girls, or you’d instantly become a gay two-year-old. Why would anyone pick blue over pink? Pink is obviously a better color. Everyone’s born confident, and everything’s taken away from you. So many people try to put their personality on someone else.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Fri, 06 Mar 2015 04:19:00 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:4:\"Matt\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:18;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:93:\"WPTavern: WPWeekly Episode 182 – John James Jacoby Talks BuddyPress, bbPress, and GlotPress\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:44:\"http://wptavern.com?p=40127&preview_id=40127\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:97:\"http://wptavern.com/wpweekly-episode-182-john-james-jacoby-talks-buddypress-bbpress-and-glotpress\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:2988:\"</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://jjj.me/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>John James Jacoby</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is nearly halfway through his</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/buddypress-bbpress-and-glotpress-development-campaign-is-now-fully-funded%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>six month development cycle</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">on</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://buddypress.org/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>BuddyPress</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">,</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://bbpress.org/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>bbPress</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, and</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://blog.glotpress.org/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>GlotPress</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. So,</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://marcuscouch.com/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Marcus Couch</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">and I invited him on the show to give us an update on how things are progressing. On average, WordPress Weekly is an hour-long. This episode however, is two hours and nine minutes, but is filled with deep conversations surrounding each project.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">In this episode, we learn the history of BuddyPress and how its connection to WordPress MU (WordPress Multisite), influenced the project’s direction. Jacoby explains what GlotPress is and why its a cornerstone of the WordPress project. We discuss the future of comments on the web and the role bbPress can play in turning things around. Last but not least, we discuss whether Jacoby’s successful crowdfunding campaign has opened the door for others who need funding to work on open source projects.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:15.428571701049805pt;"><b>Plugins Picked By Marcus:</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/buddypress-identicons/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>BuddyPress Identicons</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">automatically replaces default avatars with GitHub-style identicons. Each member’s identicon is likely to be unique, because it’s generated from a hash of their username.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/buddypress-cover-photo/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>BuddyPress Cover Photo</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">allows users to upload a cover photo to their profile.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/friends-for-bbpress/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>Friends For bbPress</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">allows users to add friends in bbPress forums. This plugin creates a section on each user’s profile page that contains their friends.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:15.428571701049805pt;"><b>WPWeekly Meta:</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;"><b>Next Episode:</b></span> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Wednesday, March 11th 9:30 P.M. Eastern</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;"><b>Subscribe To WPWeekly Via Itunes:</b></span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://itunes.apple.com/us/podcast/wordpress-weekly/id694849738%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Click here to subscribe</u></a>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>

<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>

<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;"><b>Listen To Episode #182:</b></span> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Fri, 06 Mar 2015 01:22:57 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Jeff Chandler\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:19;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:86:\"WPTavern: Wocker: Create a Docker-Based WordPress Development Environment in 3 Seconds\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=40090\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:95:\"http://wptavern.com/wocker-create-a-docker-based-wordpress-development-environment-in-3-seconds\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:5731:\"</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.jpeg)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">If you haven’t caught on to the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://www.docker.com/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Docker</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">craze, it might be time to see what it’s all about. Fans of the open source container technology appreciate that it’s lightweight, super fast to boot up, and easy to share containers through the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://registry.hub.docker.com/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Docker Hub</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Docker standardizes an app platform and its dependencies so you can hand the box over to another party without worrying about conflicting dependencies or differences between machines. While virtual machines can be rather weighty with an application plus an entire guest operating system, Docker is much lighter, containing just the application and its dependencies.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>Introducing Wocker</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.vagrantup.com/blog/vagrant-1-6.html%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>Vagrant 1.6.0</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, released last May, introduced support for Docker-based development environments, enabling Vagrant to manage them within Docker-powered Linux containers, instead of virtual machines.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">WordPress developers who want to incorporate Docker into their workflow now have a new tool at their disposal.</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wckr.github.io/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Wocker</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is a Docker-based rapid development environment of WordPress.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">If you already have Vagrant, VirtualBox, and the vagrant-hostsupdater plugin installed, then getting started with Wocker takes next to no time. Simply clone the Wocker repository:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">$ git clone https://github.com/wckr/wocker.git && cd wocker</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Then run vagrant up.  You can now navigate to your Wocker development site at:</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wocker.dev/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>http://wocker.dev/</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. Here you’ll find the latest version of WordPress installed with the default theme active.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Running a new Wocker container takes just three seconds:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">core@wocker ~ $ wocker run --name wp</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The tasks of creating a new container, restarting a stopped container, or shutting down, can all be done in a matter of seconds with</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wckr.github.io/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Wocker commands</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>Kite Koga on Creating Wocker</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Japanese WordPress developer</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://twitter.com/ixkaito%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Kite Koga</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">created Wocker to speed up his own development. Koga is also the organizer of the upcoming</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://www.facebook.com/WordCampKansai2015%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WordCamp Kansai 2015</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“I used to use MAMP and VCCW for developing on WordPress,” Koga said. “MAMP is simple and easy but I have to download or copy WordPress core and create a database every time.</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://vccw.cc/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>VCCW</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is a great tool, and I still use it now and then. It has a lot of options and functions but takes awhile to provision.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Koga also experimented with using</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://github.com/Varying-Vagrant-Vagrants/VVV%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>VVV</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">before creating Wocker. “Maybe VVV is good for developing WordPress core, but I feel it’s not fit for developing on WordPress. It takes too long to provision every time,” he said.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“Ultimately, I found that Docker was a good choice for me,” Koga said. “Once I have a Docker image, it takes only three seconds for every new WordPress container. However, Docker is a little tricky, and the command line is complex. Therefore, I made the Wocker command line to run containers and sync files with local more easily.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Wocker is intentionally simple and limited to just a handful of options, as Koga’s primary objective was to make it super fast to create a new WordPress development environment. One drawback is that you cannot run two or more containers at the same time, but it takes just seconds to switch between containers.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Koga summarized the main reasons that he opted to use Vagrant to manage Docker deployments:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
1. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
2. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">I could write some provision scripts in the Vagrantfile, so users only have to do `$ vagrant up`</span>
3. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
4. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">It was easier to map hostnames to IP addresses.</span>
5. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
6. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Vagrant with CoreOS was simpler to manage Docker images and containers than boot2docker.</span>
7. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
8. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">To sync files between local machine, virtual machine, and the Docker container was tricky, so I made Wocker commands to make it easier.</span>
9. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">If you want to test out Wocker and find that it’s not for you, it’s easy and fast to uninstall. Simply run $ vagrant destroy to remove the Wocker folder, and your local machine will always be clean.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">I tested Wocker and found that it was insanely fast to create new containers (as well as restart existing ones), a task for which I would require an extra utility (such as</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/variable-vvv-a-new-vvv-site-creation-wizard-for-wordpress%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Variable VVV</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">) to perform with VVV. If you find VVV to be too slow and want to check out an alternative,</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wckr.github.io/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Wocker</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">provides a faster way to set up simple development environments.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Fri, 06 Mar 2015 00:52:38 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Sarah Gooding\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:20;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:68:\"WPTavern: WordCamp Europe Now Taking Applications for 2016 Host City\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=40057\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:78:\"http://wptavern.com/wordcamp-europe-now-taking-applications-for-2016-host-city\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:4150:\"</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.jpeg)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Organizing an event the size of</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://europe.wordcamp.org/2015/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WordCamp Europe</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is a year-round endeavor. The 2015 event is just four months away, speaker applications are closed, and the organization team is already asking for applications for the next host city. So far, the camp has been held in Leiden and Sofia, and will come to Seville in June.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Applying to host WordCamp Europe is similar to applying to be an Olympic city. Local WordPress communities in potential host cities must submit applications, including a budget and a convincing plan. Organizers scrutinize the applications for the following factors:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Preparation of the budget and venue research</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Strength of the local WordPress community</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The merits of the new location, as compared to the previous year</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Affordability for attendees</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Potential travel difficulties</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">This year</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/seville-spain-to-host-wordcamp-europe-2015%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Seville, Spain was selected as the host city</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">after a short bidding process. WordCamp Central requested the event be scheduled earlier in the year to avoid calendar conflicts. This was an unusual turn of events but organizers are committed to re-instituting a public bidding process for all future events.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>WordCamp Europe to Pilot New Organizer Mentorship Program</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">For months in advance, an all-star lineup of WordCamp organizers from around Europe put their heads together, sharing their experiences to plan the best event possible.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“Organizing WordCamp Europe is both a pleasure and a challenge,” co-organizer Petya Raykovska told the Tavern. “What’s great about it is that you get to work with experienced WordCamp organizers from across Europe. Each organizer brings their own knowledge and perspective to the organizing team which makes it a fantastic opportunity to learn and grow.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The event has traditionally highlighted the diversity of the European WordPress community and its</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/wordpress-beyond-boundaries-a-recap-of-wordcamp-europe-2014%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>attendees’ eagerness to connect beyond boundaries</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“It’s a challenge because each of us has our own way of doing things and we have to learn how to listen to each other and compromise,” Raykovska said. “And, of course, there are all of those idioms that don’t cross language and cultural barriers!”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">New WordCamp Europe organizers should be equipped with a solid grasp of diplomacy and the ability to work with others across cultural differences.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">This year the current organization team plans to experiment with a mentorship program that would prepare the next crop of organizers to take the helm in 2016.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“We’re introducing a new process that we hope will help not only find the best team for next year, but will also be a pilot for a mentorship program for future WordCamp Europe (or any large 600+ people WordPress event) events,” Raykovska said.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“So what we want to do in 2015 is choose the team for 2016 and get them to work with us for the 2015 edition, so they can get to know what it takes, get introduced to the processes, work closely with the existing team and monitor what’s required of the local team.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Current organizers are prioritizing mentoring new additions in order to create a seamless transition from one organizational team to the next. Instead of learning the ropes at the last minute, new organizers will have the opportunity to see how it works without all of the pressure.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“We believe it will be highly beneficial for them and will ensure smooth sailing for next year’s organisation,” Raykovska said.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Thu, 05 Mar 2015 19:32:03 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Sarah Gooding\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:21;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:100:\"WPTavern: New Theme Development Company Makes First Sale Minutes After Being Approved on ThemeForest\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=40045\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:110:\"http://wptavern.com/new-theme-development-company-makes-first-sale-minutes-after-being-approved-on-themeforest\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:4372:\"</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://warriorsofcode.com/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>Warriors Of Code</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is a new WordPress theme development shop in Australia. An employee who goes by the name</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.reddit.com/user/genesisfan%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Genesisfan</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">on Reddit,</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.reddit.com/r/Wordpress/comments/2xs8l2/just_had_our_first_wordpress_theme_accepted_at/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>published a post</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">explaining how the company recently had its first theme accepted on ThemeForest and was willing to answer questions others had about the experience. According to the post, he spent the better part of six months with a designer he hired while working a full-time job developing</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://themeforest.net/item/broadsword-a-wordpress-theme-to-share-stories/full_screen_preview/10514496%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Broadsword</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">BroadSword Single Page View\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">When asked what he thought of the ThemeForest submission and review process, he responded, “We were pleasantly surprised with how quickly they turned around our review, and the level of detail they provided in their soft rejection. Aside from some technicalities that we’d missed (being more specific about what features we supported), the biggest issue was that we were missing some data validation in our files. Make sure you use the esc_ and sanitize_ functions provided by WordPress!”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Once the issues were addressed, ThemeForest approved the submission and the team made its first sale within minutes of it going live. When asked what makes their theme different in the marketplace, he responded, “We kept the theme options to a minimum. We were both pretty tired of themes that include a thousand options and tend to be more like frameworks than standalone themes.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Based on</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/envato-continues-to-rake-in-the-cash-from-wordpress-themes-packaged-as-complete-website-solutions%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>stats that highlight</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">how well ThemeForest is doing, it’s not surprising that Warriors of code made their first sale within minutes of going live. However, the company used social media to its advantage, so it’s possible one their followers purchased the theme based on tweets. In fact, the company explains how they handled promotion:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Regarding promotion, we’ve been tweeting it out and liking the facebook page we’ve set up and luckily, it’s now trending on ThemeForest. It helps that I’m on the east coast of Canada and my partner is in Sydney, Australia, so we’re able to pretty much cover comment replies quickly at any time of the day. I think that goes a long way to helping promote a positive vibe about the theme.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">After 24 hours, the company has six sales at $43 each. It’s not a huge amount, but it’s a start. The big takeaways is that ThemeForest reviews are catching insecure coding practices and making the first sale is a quick endeavor, especially if you already have a social media presence. The company</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.reddit.com/r/Wordpress/comments/2xs8l2/just_had_our_first_wordpress_theme_accepted_at/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>answers several other questions</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">related to the experience.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">We know that in this instance, ThemeForest did its job to discover insecure coding practices during reviews. It’s also refreshing to hear from an up and coming theme company that they’re tired of theme options. I haven’t used their product so I can’t confirm if its claims are true, but it’s a step in the right direction to see other ThemeForest sellers make such statements in public.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Thu, 05 Mar 2015 07:33:10 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Jeff Chandler\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:22;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:21:\"Matt: WIRED Re-launch\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:21:\"http://ma.tt/?p=44764\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:37:\"http://ma.tt/2015/03/wired-re-launch/\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:2726:\"</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">There’s the smart publishers, and then there’s the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://ma.tt/2015/02/developer-employment-act/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>ones going out of business</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. WIRED is one of the smart ones, and just launched an awesome redesign on WordPress.</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.wired.com/2015/03/our-new-site/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>From their editor-in-chief</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Back in 1994 we launched Hotwired, the first site with original editorial content created for the web. It was a digital home for reporting on the future of science, business, design, and technology. You’ve come to trust us over the past two decades, but our growth online has sometimes come too quickly and with some pain. When I took over as editor in chief in 2012, WIRED had an archive of more than 100,000 stories. That’s good! But they were spread out over more than a dozen different databases, sections, and homepages tenuously connected by virtual duct tape and chewing gum. The cleanup process—onerous and without a shred of glamour—took almost 15 months. But finally, last year, our engineers rolled out a newly unified site architecture built atop a single streamlined WordPress installation. And you didn’t notice a hiccup. Maybe you saw that pages loaded a touch faster. Stories looked more WIRED.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The story of</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.wired.com/2015/03/wired-dot-com-from-the-devs/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>the engineering behind it from Kathleen Vignos is also cool</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The redesign gives us the third incarnation of our Curator application, which started years ago as a separate Groovy on Grails application maintained by a single Java developer. Curator once consumed articles from 35 different blogs for curation on our homepage. When we migrated our 17 active WordPress blogs into one WordPress install, we also rewrote Curator in Cake PHP to match our WordPress PHP backend. After this, anyone on our team could maintain Curator—but the architecture remained the same and lived outside of WordPress. Using this version of Curator, our web producer team manually constructed the homepage throughout each day as various stories were ready to be promoted.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Our new and improved Curator is now a custom WordPress plugin—and it’s artificially intelligent! This allows our homepage and section landing pages to be both automated and curated at the same time. Stories flow through automagically based on editorial criteria, but editors can take control of the flow by locking stories in certain slots in our card system. This means our homepage and section landing pages are constantly changing with new stories all day long.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Curator sounds cool, as does the coming “longform feature article builder.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Thu, 05 Mar 2015 02:36:00 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:4:\"Matt\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:23;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:67:\"WPTavern: Ninja Forms 2.9 Ships With Major Performance Improvements\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=39995\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:77:\"http://wptavern.com/ninja-forms-2-9-ships-with-major-performance-improvements\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:5648:\"</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Ninja Forms</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://ninjaforms.com/version-2-9/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>2.9 is available</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">and features an improved user experience along with major performance enhancements. One of the biggest performance problems 2.9 solves is handling large forms. Prior to 2.9, users had to edit a php.ini file to handle the increased amount of server resources needed to process long forms, something many shared webhosts don’t allow. According to Kevin Stover, lead developer of Ninja Forms, 2.9 not only solves this problem, but the form builder is more efficient overall.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">In our local tests, a 578 field form was 12.8mb and took 33.52 seconds to load. (It also occasionally crashed our browser.) In version 2.9, the same form was only 1.2mb and took only 1.41 seconds to load. We call that progress.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The user experience is vastly improved compared to earlier versions of the plugin. Now when you edit an existing form or want to create a new one, you’re taken to the form builder instead of a page filled with confusing settings. After installing Ninja Forms, it took less than five minutes to recreate the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/contact-me%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Tavern’s contact form</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Ninja Forms 2.9 User Interface\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">A small but noticeable change is that, when you create a new form without a submit button and save it, a model window pops up reminding you to add one. Or, you can let Ninja Forms add it automatically. It’s hard to make a form useful without a submit button!</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Ninja Forms 2.9 Submit Button Reminder\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">One of the major changes to the form building experience is configuring fields. In previous versions of Ninja Forms, all of the configuration options for fields were in view which felt overwhelming. In Ninja Forms 2.9, field options are hidden behind drop down menus that are closed by default. This allows you to configure them at your own pace.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Ninja Forms 2.9 Field Settings\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Overall, Ninja Forms 2.9 is a solid release and offers a better experience than its predecessors. I found it easier to build forms without having to rely on documentation. Stover says this release, “lays the groundwork for even better stuff to come down the road.” You can download Ninja Forms free from the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/ninja-forms/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WordPress plugin directory</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">To learn more about James Laws, Co-founder of WP Ninjas and his company,</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/wpweekly-episode-179-interview-with-james-laws-co-founder-of-wp-ninjas%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>listen to episode 179</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">of WordPress Weekly. In the show, we discuss some of the improvements that made it into Ninja Forms 2.9.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/EPISODE-179-Interview-With-James-Laws-Co-Founder-of-WP-Ninjas.mp3%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>http://wptavern.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/EPISODE-179-Interview-With-James-Laws-Co-Founder-of-WP-Ninjas.mp3</u></a>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Run Time 1:17:51</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Artist Jeff Chandler and Marcus Couch</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Album WordPress Weekly</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Track 179</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">File Name</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/02/EPISODE-179-Interview-With-James-Laws-Co-Founder-of-WP-Ninjas.mp3%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>EPISODE-179-Interview-With-James-Laws-Co-Founder-of-WP-Ninjas.mp3</u></a>

<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Thu, 05 Mar 2015 00:32:19 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Jeff Chandler\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:24;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:88:\"WPTavern: BuddyPress 2.3 Development Kicks Off, Contributors Prioritize Work on New APIs\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=39890\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:97:\"http://wptavern.com/buddypress-2-3-development-kicks-off-contributors-prioritize-work-on-new-apis\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:2810:\"</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.jpeg)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The BuddyPress 2.3 development cycle is now in full swing, following a successful 2.2.1 maintenance release that</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/buddypress-breaks-one-day-download-record-with-2-2-1-release%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>broke the plugin’s one-day download record</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">with more than 10,000 downloads in just 24 hours. More minor fixes are on deck for inclusion in the forthcoming 2.2.2 release.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">This week contributors identified priorities for new features and improvements to work on for the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://bpdevel.wordpress.com/2015/03/03/bp-2-3-0-dev-cycle-contributor-priorities/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>2.3 development cycle</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. Updates to BuddyPress’ existing APIs and work on the following new APIs commenced this week:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://buddypress.trac.wordpress.org/ticket/6210%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>New Invitations API</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">– a flexible API to create/retrieve invitations across components</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://buddypress.trac.wordpress.org/ticket/5429%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>New Attachments API</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">– a new component to manage BuddyPress attachments</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://github.com/paulgibbs/buddypress/tree/posts2posts%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Possible new Relationships API</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">– for the Favorites/Likes features</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Improvements to Member Types API –</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://buddypress.trac.wordpress.org/ticket/5192%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>ability to create member-type-specific xprofile fields</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, fixes for existing bugs</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Notifications API –</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://buddypress.trac.wordpress.org/ticket/6257%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>add a metadata table</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">– useful for storing additional data outside the available notification table’s schema</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">New APIs would make it possible for BuddyPress developers to build extensions that bring in more exciting features, such as a core-supported way to manage media/user galleries, local avatar management, invitation capabilities for groups, sites, blogs, docs, etc. The APIs give developers a way to custom tailor those experiences for their communities.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">While none of these APIs and improvements are yet guaranteed to make it into 2.3, contributors have hammered out the tickets they will be investing in during the next three months. The official release is currently targeted for the end of May, 2015. To follow progress on tickets for the 2.3 milestone, check out the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://buddypress.trac.wordpress.org/milestone/2.3%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>roadmap</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">on BuddyPress trac.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Wed, 04 Mar 2015 22:10:21 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Sarah Gooding\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:25;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:75:\"Post Status: Adii Pienaar is making a WordPress comeback with stake in Obox\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:30:\"https://poststatus.com/?p=9009\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:73:\"https://poststatus.com/adii-pienaar-making-wordpress-comeback-stake-obox/\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:6289:\"</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Adii Pienaar has been largely away from the WordPress community for much of the last year and a half. He’s one of three co-founders of WooThemes and was instrumental in</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.woothemes.com/about/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>their growth</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">and success they achieved from 2008 until his departure in late 2013.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Today, Adii is making the second step of his WordPress comeback. In addition to</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://receiptful.com/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Receiptful</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">— his new eCommerce receipts product — he’s taking on an advisory role with Obox, to go along with a cash investment in the company.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://oboxthemes.com/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>Obox</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is based in Cape Town, South Africa — where Adii and WooThemes are also based. Obox has been around the block as well.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">They were founded in 2009 and are lead by brothers Marc and David Perel. Obox has experienced times of great success — peaking as a team of 8 in 2012 — and also years where they’ve scaled back in response to more competition and watering down of the WordPress theme market.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Before Adii left WooThemes, he had numerous conversations with Obox about an acquisition, but the parties could never agree on the specifics.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>Adii now owns a 30% stake in Obox</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">David Perel showed Adii some screenshots of Layers while they were wrapping up development of</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://poststatus.com/layers-theme-obox/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>the new product</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. Adii was intrigued and they started once again talking about joining forces, except this time the direction changed; they started talking about what it would look like for Adii to join Obox.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Adii has made a cash investment in the Obox team, in return for a 30% stake in the company. The investment gives Obox a valuation in the millions of dollars, “but less than $10 million.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The cash from Adii’s investment is largely going to be used for operating expenses for the Obox team as they create the business model around Layers. Obox has also beefed up their team by</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://blog.oboxthemes.com/calyx-joins-obox/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>acquihiring Calyx</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, a two man Cape Town agency.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">According to David, “Every cent Obox raises and makes will go into Layers.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>Roller coaster ride</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Adii has been on a bit of a roller coaster ride since his departure of WooThemes. I’ve heard both Adii and his cofounders (Magnus Jepson and Mark Forrester) describe their split as a divorce. It was a hard time.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The split was complete, and Adii released all ownership of the company for an amount he has</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://mixergy.com/interviews/adii-pienaar-public-beta-interview/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>confirmed on Mixergy</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">was seven figures. He tells me that he has been fully paid for his shares.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">With cash in hand, Adii had room to take some risks, and with that risk came a mixture of successes and failures. His first foray into another product was Public Beta, which had many iterations before he ultimately deemed it a lost cause.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">His latest startup seems to have traction; Receiptful has had a successful launch, is getting nice adoption, and is expanding to multiple eCommerce platforms after an initial WooCommerce-only launch.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Time heals all wounds, and it appears Adii’s relationship with WooThemes is also mended. They even</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.woothemes.com/2015/02/why-customized-receipts-increase-ecommerce-revenue/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>blogged about Receiptful</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">recently on the main WooThemes blog.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>Renewed passion for WordPress</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Both Receiptful and the Obox investment show renewed passion for WordPress, as well as a sign of Adii getting back to his roots and what he knows best. With the launch of WooCommerce, Adii spearheaded what became a huge success during his time at WooThemes; and WooCommerce has only further grown since his departure.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Adii hopes to take what he’s learned — both at WooThemes and with his adventures since — to his role at Obox.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">One of the biggest challenges within WordPress is the disconnect between how developers and end-users use it, which makes building great WordPress products really hard. It’s also something that we encountered often at Woo and instead of truly tackling the problem we leaned towards building tools for developers.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Layers is different in that sense, because it’s focused on the (end-)user experience from the ground up. I couldn’t be more excited to work with David & Marc to grow Layers, as they’re fanatical about UX and it’s my belief that they’ll finally make progress to closing the gap between a developer tool and end-user product.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">And in terms of the commercialisation of Layers… Well, let’s just say that I see opportunities and patterns that were prevalent in WooCommerce’s early days too…</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>A new step for a dynamic WordPress figure</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Adii was a huge and dynamic figure in the early days of WordPress’ commercial product space. He’s always made bold decisions — some good and some not so good.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">He has a penchant to make quick decisions and he iterates at a rapid pace; to some it can be off-putting, but for finding a hit it can be hugely important. In contrast, the Obox team makes calculated risks. While they’ve done a great deal of interesting work and experimentation on their own, they have largely stuck with the theme business while some of their early theme competitors rotated toward plugins and other verticals.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">I believe that the combination of Adii and the Perel brothers will make for a compelling trio of leadership at the helm for Obox. Their Layers launch</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://blog.oboxthemes.com/what-hitting-the-top-spot-on-product-hunt-did-to-our-launch-day/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>certainly made waves</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, and their next steps will be hugely important for the future of a company that has gone all in on a product without a monetization strategy.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">You can read the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://blog.oboxthemes.com/adii-co-founder-of-woothemes-and-woocommerce-has-joined-obox/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>official announcement on the Obox blog</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Wed, 04 Mar 2015 15:56:41 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:15:\"Brian Krogsgard\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:26;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:56:\"WPTavern: WordPress Plugin Directory Launches New Design\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=39957\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:66:\"http://wptavern.com/wordpress-plugin-directory-launches-new-design\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:3778:\"</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The WordPress.org Meta team is on a roll this month. Following the successful</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/wordpress-theme-directory-launches-new-design%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>launch of the new theme directory</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>plugin directory</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is getting the same treatment with</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://make.wordpress.org/meta/2015/03/04/new-plugin-directory-theme/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>a fresh coat of paint</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">and a set of brand new features.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Browsing the official plugin directory is now similar to searching via the admin plugin browser. Having all of this code on hand made it easier for the meta team to replicate the experience in the directory.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">In addition to the new design, the directory includes a new section for logged-in users to manage favorited plugins. Previously, users had to navigate to their own profile pages to access this information. With more than 36,000 listings in the directory, favorites are becoming an important feature for users who want to keep track of plugins they use frequently.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The “Popular” section seems to be populated by extensions with the highest number of active installs. It would be helpful to be able to further sort popular plugins based on different criteria, i.e. the most-favorited plugins and those with the highest ratings.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/browse/beta/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>Beta Testing</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is a new section which you may recognize from the WordPress admin. It lists all the feature plugins that are currently under consideration for inclusion in core at some point in the future. This more prominent display will help users discover the plugins, resulting in an increase in feedback for contributors.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Users can also now search for plugins based on author, keyword, or tag. Searching is lightning fast, but it could be improved with filtering options to further narrow down the results.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Although individual plugin pages did not receive a design update, they now reflect more accurate data with the number of active installs for each plugin. This provides plugin authors with a better understanding of how many sites are actively using that functionality, as opposed to just having downloaded it once and then uninstalled it.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The new design is more visually-oriented than the previous one, making it easier for users to quickly scan through a long list of results. It is now more important than ever for developers to prioritize</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/wordpress-4-0-adds-custom-icons-to-the-plugin-installer%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>plugin branding</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">if they want their work to stand out in the official directory.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
