| <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://trends.builtwith.com/cms/WPTouch-Pro%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WPTouch (Pro Data only)</u></a> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">15,696</span> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">410,000+</span> |
| <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://trends.builtwith.com/widgets/bbPress%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>bbPress</u></a> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">126,081</span> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">250,000+</span> |
| <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://trends.builtwith.com/widgets/MailPoet%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>MailPoet</u></a> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">149,018</span> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">230,000+</span> |
| <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://trends.builtwith.com/widgets/The-Events-Calendar%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>The Events Calendar</u></a> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">56,095</span> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">230,000+</span> |
| <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://trends.builtwith.com/cms/BuddyPress%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>BuddyPress</u></a> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">76,853</span> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">150,000+</span> |
| <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://trends.builtwith.com/shop/WP-eCommerce%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WP eCommerce</u></a> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">58,115</span> | <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:12pt;">80,000+</span> |


<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>Data Accuracy</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">There are zero instances from this list where BuiltWith shows more sites than WordPress.org using a plugin. That is a confidence enhancing note, as WordPress.org should be much more fully comprehensive.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">There are some instances, especially with plugins that are on the backend, or have limited front-end visibility, where the numbers don’t line up too well.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">However, for the highly visible plugins, the WordPress.org data</span> <span style="font-family:Courier-Oblique;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;"><i>feels</i></span> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">good to me, in comparison to BuiltWith.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The team working on the new stats has less confidence in the numbers as the numbers get higher. So for plugins with well beyond a million installs, they have reason to believe adoption may be different than what is reflected in the stats.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>How stats are collected</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">WordPress.org collects this data via the update checks that a WordPress install performs.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">There are a couple of caveats to consider for the WordPress.org data:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The numbers could include (and therefore inflate numbers) non-public (dev and staging environments) domains. According to Andrew Nacin, the team does exclude sites with “localhost”, “.dev”, “.local” and “other telltale signs of local development and staging sites, like IP addresses.”</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The numbers don’t include websites that have, via code, turned off update checks.</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Some data is making more sense as it is collected. Now that it’s a few months old, they are able to make better determinations about what is accurate and what is not.</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Keep in mind the percentage of installs that wouldn’t be included in these checks is minimal. Also, unfortunately, a large percentage of folks just don’t keep dev or local installs of their websites, so those numbers probably do not inflate the totals as much as you may think either — though I’m guessing on that front.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">For the accuracy of the numbers, the team has seen some discrepancies, where one graph shows data that should also impact another, but it does not. They’re working to resolve these issues still.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>This data is important</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The team working on this project has been deciding on which stats pages to showcase for the general public, and which to show to plugin authors.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The active install data is important, and it needs to be public for users, in addition to plugin authors. If we can ensure that the data is even remotely accurate, it is far superior to using download counts alone.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Download counts give legacy plugins too much clout in the repo, and also make misinformation all the more likely.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">A prime example is that of Slimstats, which had the recent security breach. That plugin has over 1.3 million downloads. But when I looked at the stats, there were only 100,000 or so active installs.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Yet, when tech blogs covered the security breach of the plugin, they largely cited “over a million” installs at risk. This is bad for WordPress and it’s unnecessary. We can fix it.</span> <span style="font-family:Courier-Oblique;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;"><i>We have the data</i></span><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Lead developer Dion Hulse also had this to say about the progress of the stats pages, when I notified him that I had access to these numbers:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The current beta stats page was put together as a trial to find what data plugin authors wanted (We’ve been in touch with a few who have provided great feedback in the past on the stats).</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Based on what they have learned, they are launching the active install numbers and active version chart, and will follow up with a future launch for the plugin author only stats. The plugin author stats are going to go live with the notice that they may not be totally accurate, and they may change over time as the team continues to analyze the data.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">WordPress is open source, and has absolutely massive adoption across the web, as we all know. It is therefore a source for what’s become a multi-billion dollar industry. WordPress.org and those that hold the keys to the website have great power.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Changes to the website and how themes, plugins, and any other potentially business-centric data make a difference to real businesses.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">All those businesses have signed up for this (being involved with the WordPress project), and therefore any risks associated, but in my mind this data — especially of active installs — brings welcome sunlight to a murky plugin landscape.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">I’m glad I was able to see this data. I’m very pleased that the active install numbers are replacing the download counts. WordPress itself will be better for it.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Sat, 28 Feb 2015 02:38:12 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:15:\"Brian Krogsgard\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:42;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:78:\"WPTavern: Time To Abolish Post Specific Metaboxes in The WordPress Post Editor\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=39792\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:74:\"http://wptavern.com/time-to-abolish-metaboxes-in-the-wordpress-post-editor\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:3615:\"</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">photo credit:</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.flickr.com/photos/66992990@N00/6532917427%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>packing up</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">–</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>(license)</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">I’ve used WordPress to write about WordPress for more than seven years, it’s how I make a living. Recently though, writing in WordPress feels more like being a data entry specialist. I guess in some ways, it’s not surprising considering that’s exactly what I’m doing.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The post editor is more or less a pretty user interface that enables me to add data to a database. I think the feeling is stronger if you write in WordPress every day as it’s an endless cycle of filling in text areas, fields, uploading media, and clicking the publish button. Many of these tasks take place within their own metabox which is something that detracts from a seamless experience. I don’t know about you, but I’m ready to try something different.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Many of the posts I write post consists of content blocks such as: blockquotes, media, etc. Thinking about how I create content in WordPress, the conceptual post editor</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://make.wordpress.org/design/2013/08/08/proposal-improving-the-content-editing-experience/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>proposed in 2013</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">makes complete sense. Instead of a full-blown TinyMCE editor, I could select from a set of established content blocks. In addition to the content block idea, some of the metaboxes are integrated into the editor.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Proposal To Revamp The Post Writing/Editing Interface\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The publish, categories, and tags metaboxes are integrated into the editor. It probably wouldn’t take too much effort to add a way to feature an image. I don’t know how usable this interface is, but I’d love to give it a try for a few months. The more metaboxes that disappear, the less I feel like I’m hitting switches and turning knobs before clicking the publish button. By having some of these tasks integrated into the editor, I think it’ll provide a better publishing experience.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">I probably wouldn’t be able to get rid of all the metaboxes because of the plugins I use. However, I’m most interested in an interface that consists of less scrolling, searching, etc., that gets me to the publishing stage quicker without feeling like a data entry specialist.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Despite a large amount of discussion surrounding the proposal, the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://make.wordpress.org/design/2013/08/08/proposal-improving-the-content-editing-experience/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>concept lost steam</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. Although I’ve never used it, I feel like I’d prefer it over the current editor. It’s anyone’s guess if we’ll ever see the post editor undergo a major change, but if it did, this is the direction I’d like to see it go.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Sat, 28 Feb 2015 02:28:08 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Jeff Chandler\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:43;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:94:\"WPTavern: SIDEKICK Adds Support For WordPress Multisite, Launches Partnership With MediaTemple\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=39720\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:103:\"http://wptavern.com/sidekick-adds-support-for-wordpress-multisite-launches-partnership-with-mediatemple\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:6344:\"</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">SIDEKICK has</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.sidekick.pro/blog/multisite-genesis-media-temple-oh-my/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>announced</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">that its voice-guided</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/sidekick/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WordPress training video plugin</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">now supports WordPress Multisite. According to SIDEKICK Co-founder, Ben Fox, WordPress Multisite has been one of the most requested features from users. Users can now:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
1. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
2. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Easily auto activate your SIDEKICK license across all sites on your network</span>
3. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
4. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Set a Walkthrough to auto-start the first time a new user logs into any site on your network</span>
5. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
6. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Enable and disable walkthroughs and walkthrough categories network wide from one screen</span>
7. <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">SIDEKICK has a</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://support.sidekick.pro/article/101-sidekick-for-wordpress-multisite%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>support article</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">that goes into more detail on how to activate Multisite.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:15.428571701049805pt;"><b>Challenges Encountered</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">SIDEKICK’s Chief of Technology and Product Development, Bart Dabek, describes the most challenging aspect they encountered adding Multisite support, “The biggest challenge was incorporating our API and building out the relationship between the super admin setup page and individual sites. Once that relationship was established, it was easy to pull in settings from the network settings and assign them to individual sites.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“Some things to keep in mind with our particular situation is, keeping authentication details within the super admin account so that if our sessions expire, the system can automatically log back into our API and continue to auto activate SIDEKICK on new sites without the need for the super admin to do anything. It’s something developers will have to consider if they’re dealing with an API on this level,” Dabek said.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:15.428571701049805pt;"><b>Free Genesis Walkthroughs</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">If you use SIDEKICK with the Genesis Framework by</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.studiopress.com/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>StudioPress</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, you’ll see 30 free Genesis walkthroughs. As long as you’re using a Genesis powered theme, you’ll see the videos appear in the SIDEKICK drawer. The walkthroughs were</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.web-savvy-marketing.com/2015/02/genesis-walkthroughs/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>created by Web Savvy Marketing</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, a WordPress development agency that specializes in Genesis themes.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:15.428571701049805pt;"><b>MediaTemple Learns From GoDaddy’s Mistake</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.sidekick.pro/plans/wordpress-basics/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>basic student plan</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">offered by SIDEKICK priced at $5 per month, is available for free to MediaTemple customers. In addition to 30 Genesis walkthroughs,  customers gain access to 160+ WordPress walkthroughs. We’ve received a few reports from readers that SIDEKICK has been activated on every site on MediaTemple without an opt-in notice, similar to what happened to</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/without-notifying-customers-godaddy-and-mediatemple-auto-activate-sidekick%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>GoDaddy customers late in 2014</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. However, this isn’t the case.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Email send to MediaTemple Customers about SIDEKICK\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">MediaTemple emailed customers on February 23rd announcing the partnership and included directions on how to activate the plugin. It appears as though the company learned from</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/without-notifying-customers-godaddy-and-mediatemple-auto-activate-sidekick%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>GoDaddy’s mistake</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">How to turn on SIDEKICK\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Around the same time the email went out to customers, the company sent out a press release to the media with the same information.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">MediaTemple SIDEKICK press release\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Multisite support gives network administrators the ability to easily provide access to an educational tool. Through strategic partnerships with SIDEKICK, MediaTemple, and GoDaddy, customers have an easy way to learn the ins and outs of WordPress.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">While some</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/without-notifying-customers-godaddy-and-mediatemple-auto-activate-sidekick#comment-62200%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>don’t like the approach</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">taken by SIDEKICK, it’s another option to those who find it difficult to use the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://codex.wordpress.org/Main_Page%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WordPress Codex</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">or other educational resources.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Sat, 28 Feb 2015 00:42:22 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Jeff Chandler\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:44;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:57:\"WPTavern: Clean Up WordPress with the New WP-Sweep Plugin\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=39753\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:67:\"http://wptavern.com/clean-up-wordpress-with-the-new-wp-sweep-plugin\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:4395:\"</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.jpeg)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">After years of creating new content, changing themes, and adding and removing plugins, a WordPress installation can become littered with unused, orphaned, and duplicated data. Not every plugin properly extricates itself and its data from your site when you uninstall.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://profiles.wordpress.org/gamerz/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>Lester Chan</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">‘s new</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-sweep/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WP Sweep</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">plugin was designed to perform housekeeping on WordPress installations. Chan is a prolific plugin developer, who created his first plugin in 2003 shortly after WordPress was forked from b2. He now has 24+ plugins listed in directory.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">WP-Sweep’s distinguishing characteristic is that it uses proper WordPress delete functions as much as possible instead of running direct delete MySQL queries. This method is in direct contrast to similarly purposed plugins such as</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-optimize/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WP-Optimize</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, which has been downloaded more than 1.5 million times.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Looking through WP-Optimize and they are using SQL query to delete post revisions which means there will be orphaned data left behind.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">— Lester Chan (@gamerz)</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://twitter.com/gamerz/status/569742401398386688%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>February 23, 2015</u></a>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>

<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>What can WP-Sweep clean?</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">WP-Sweep uses WordPress delete functions, such as wp_delete_post_revision(), delete_post_meta(), wp_delete_comment(), etc. to clean up the database. It can perform sweeps of all of the following:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Revisions</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Auto drafts</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Deleted comments</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Unapproved comments</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Spammed comments</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Deleted comments</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Orphaned post meta</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Orphaned comment meta</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Orphaned user meta</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Orphan term relationships</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Unused terms</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Duplicated post meta</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Duplicated comment meta</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Duplicated user meta</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Transient options</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">WP-Sweep can be found under the tools menu after you install it. It will give you a full overview of items that can be cleaned up.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.jpeg)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Scroll further down and you’ll see reports for comments, users, terms, and options. Certain sweeps have extra instructions to consider before using, i.e unused terms:</span> <span style="font-family:Courier-Oblique;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;"><i>“Note that some unused terms might belong to draft posts that have not been published yet. Only sweep this when you do not have any draft posts.”</i></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">One user tested the plugin and</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://twitter.com/slaFFik/status/570536799333961728%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>reported</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">a savings of 23.5% on database size. He also reported a bug, which Chan promptly</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://github.com/lesterchan/wp-sweep/issues/1%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>fixed</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://twitter.com/gamerz%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>@gamerz</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">plugin saved me 23,5% of a database size. But not everything works as expected:</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://t.co/UeiDedH7Yd%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>https://t.co/UeiDedH7Yd</u></a>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">— Slava UA (@slaFFik)</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://twitter.com/slaFFik/status/570536799333961728%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>February 25, 2015</u></a>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>

<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Chan plans to continue to develop the plugin and is</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://twitter.com/gamerz/status/570902672636645376%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>considering adding unused options</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">to the sweeps in a future version. If you want to use the plugin to clean up your website, you are strongly advised to make a backup of your database first, as the actions it performs are irreversible. Check out</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-sweep/changelog/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WP-Sweep on WordPress.org</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">and favorite it for the next time you need to do some database housekeeping.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Fri, 27 Feb 2015 22:03:08 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Sarah Gooding\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:45;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:83:\"WPTavern: WordPress Theme Review Handbook Updated to Include Design Recommendations\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=39716\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:93:\"http://wptavern.com/wordpress-theme-review-handbook-updated-to-include-design-recommendations\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:4690:\"</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.jpeg)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">photo credit:</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.flickr.com/photos/63191453@N00/2650862549%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Artist’s Room</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">–</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>(license)</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/wordpress-theme-directory-launches-new-design%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>new design of the WordPress Theme Directory</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is a welcome change on WordPress.org. Faster browsing and filtering means users can more easily sort through the thousands of available options. While the directory includes many beautiful, high quality themes, it often requires sifting through hundreds to find that diamond in the rough.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">One of the reasons for the glut of lackluster and uninspired new theme submissions is a lack of design feedback from the Theme Review team. Reviews have traditionally focused primarily on the code quality of themes and often neglect major design issues with new submissions.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">During the team’s</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://make.wordpress.org/themes/2015/02/27/this-weeks-a-little-late-meeting-notes-we/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>meeting</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">this week, contributors discussed ways to encourage more design feedback on submissions to the directory. WordPress.org theme author</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://twitter.com/ryelle%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Kelly Dwan</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">broached the topic in a recent</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://redradar.net/2015/02/23/1612/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>post</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">about ways to get designers involved during the review process:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">If we tell people that they don’t need to be expert developers to review themes, and that this is a good way to learn better coding practices, why don’t we do the same with design? Good design is just as subjective as code standards (in that the basics aren’t, but people like to argue about it anyway).</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The quality of code in the theme repo is improved by the review process, so we should encourage design reviews to increase the quality of design, too.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Her post included practical suggestions for updating the theme review handbook to provide better guidance on how to offer design feedback. This is one way that designers can contribute on WordPress.org, even if they’re not comfortable helping with the code review aspect of the process.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>New Design Recommendation Added to the Handbook</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://twitter.com/melchoyce%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>Mel Choyce</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, Design Engineer at Automattic, volunteered to start filling out the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://make.wordpress.org/themes/handbook/review/recommended/design/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>design section of the Theme Review Handbook</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">with recommendations. “Theme authors are never exposed to design guidelines before uploading their themes, unlike code guidelines,” she said. This is something the team is currently working to change.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The design recommendations so far are formatted to help the theme author think more critically about design decisions. Instead of taking a hard line about subjective aspects of design, the recommendations invite the designer to consider the theme from a user’s perspective. A few examples include:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Can you tell if the theme has an ideal audience in mind?</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Is the type large enough to comfortably read?</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Body text should generally be 14px or larger on desktop, unless using a font with a generous x-height.</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Are the header and body fonts easy to read?</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Is there enough difference between headers and paragraphs to distinguish them from each other?</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Do paragraphs have enough line-height? If you squint your eyes, can you still see some space between lines?</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">A good rule of thumb is 1.3-1.4 on headers, and 1.4-1.6 on body text.</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Is the color contrast high enough? Is the type readable against its background?</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Do the details (drop shadows, gradients, etc.) distract at all from the content?</span>
- <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Encouraging more design feedback is difficult without offering reviewers the tools to so with confidence. The art of offering design feedback is tricky and it requires diplomacy to do it in a way that doesn’t crush the spirit of a fledgling theme author.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">A basic set of guidelines is a valuable resource that team members can refer to during the review process. If theme authors are open to the recommendations, the quality of their designs can be significantly improved for future submissions.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Fri, 27 Feb 2015 20:52:35 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Sarah Gooding\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:46;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:55:\"WPTavern: WordPress Theme Directory Launches New Design\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=39691\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:65:\"http://wptavern.com/wordpress-theme-directory-launches-new-design\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:2719:\"</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The new design for the official</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/themes/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WordPress Theme Directory</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is live today. Contributors on the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://make.wordpress.org/meta/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WordPress Meta team</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">released a</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://wptavern.com/preview-the-new-design-for-the-wordpress-themes-directory%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>preview of the design</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">in early January and worked quickly over the past month to resolve any outstanding issues related to the new plugin that powers the directory.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">In addition to an updated design, the theme repository has been moved away from bbPress and into a new plugin, which includes a custom post type for the repository package and a theme uploader.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.png)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The design is likely already familiar to most users, as it was matched to the UI to the admin themes browser. Now, whether you’re behind the wheel of your WordPress site in the admin, or browsing on the official directory, the experience of searching and sorting themes is more consistent.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Konstantin Obenland, in cooperation with Samuel “Otto” Wood, spearheaded the migration to the new plugin and design, with help and feedback from other members of the meta team. The work is not yet complete, but the new directory provides a vastly improved experience for users on the hunt for a new theme. Searching and filtering are both faster and users can quickly browse through thumbnails of all the queried results via infinite scroll.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Future internationalization improvements to the directory should make it an even better resource for WordPress’ growing global audience.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“There is still a lot more work to be done, but everything that’s remaining can be added, changed, or improved with the new directory active,” Obenland said in an update earlier this week.</span> <span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;"><b>“Most importantly we want to make it available to language sites, so that we have an internationalized theme directory, and there will be more to be done around that.”</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The improved browsing experience does a much better job of showcasing the work of theme authors, many of whom spend countless hours updating and supporting their free themes as a contribution to the WordPress community. Overall, the new design is a better representation of the high quality themes available in the official directory.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Fri, 27 Feb 2015 01:58:09 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Sarah Gooding\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:47;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:52:\"WPTavern: Add Infinite Scroll to the WordPress Admin\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=39320\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:62:\"http://wptavern.com/add-infinite-scroll-to-the-wordpress-admin\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:3059:\"</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.jpeg)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">photo credit:</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.flickr.com/photos/25066200@N02/8659691170%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:TamilSangamMN;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>தேவையில்லை</u></a> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.flickr.com/photos/25066200@N02/8659691170%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:TamilSangamMN;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>காரணங்கள்</u></a><a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.flickr.com/photos/25066200@N02/8659691170%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>,</u></a> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.flickr.com/photos/25066200@N02/8659691170%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:TamilSangamMN;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>நீ</u></a> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.flickr.com/photos/25066200@N02/8659691170%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:TamilSangamMN;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>எங்கே</u></a><a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://www.flickr.com/photos/25066200@N02/8659691170%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>?</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">–</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>(license)</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The WordPress admin list tables can get quite lengthy to browse if you’re relying on regular pagination, especially when you need to perform bulk actions.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">As of WordPress 4.0, the grid view of the media library has beautiful support for infinite scrolling, but list tables for posts and users still rely on old-fashioned pagination. Setting the screen options to include more listings can help, but this, too, can become cumbersome.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.jpeg)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://github.com/cedaro/infinite-wp-list-tables%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#00eeff;"><u>Infinite WP List Tables</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">is a new plugin created by WordPress developer</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://twitter.com/bradyvercher%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Brady Vercher</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. It’s a simple extension that adds infinite scroll to several pages within the admin, i.e. posts and users. Infinite WP List Tables has no settings to configure – simply activate it and it’s working.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.jpeg)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">I tested the plugin both on a single site and on a multisite installation with thousands of sites and users. In both scenarios the plugin performed nicely to infinitely scroll admin list tables for sites, posts, and users.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">It does not seem to work with comments, nor does it work with third party plugin tables such as the BuddyPress activity and groups admin listings. Integrations for plugins that display products, portfolio items, quotes, or other listings in the admin would make it even more useful, but I can see why Vercher might want to limit the plugin to core admin list tables.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Do you think something like this should be added to core, given that the media library works nicely with infinite scroll? As far as I’ve seen, no tickets currently exist on trac to propose infinite scroll for other areas of the admin. Do you prefer traditional pagination for sites, posts, users, and comment list tables?</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Thu, 26 Feb 2015 23:05:59 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Sarah Gooding\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:48;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:24:\"Matt: Net Neutrality Win\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:21:\"http://ma.tt/?p=44728\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:40:\"http://ma.tt/2015/02/net-neutrality-win/\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:355:\"</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">A lot of the tech news I’ve linked here has been a bit of a downer, but today we can celebrate:</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22http://arstechnica.com/business/2015/02/fcc-votes-for-net-neutrality-a-ban-on-paid-fast-lanes-and-title-ii/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>FCC votes for net neutrality, a ban on paid fast lanes, and Title II</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. This is not an outcome I would have bet on a year ago.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Thu, 26 Feb 2015 21:17:25 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:4:\"Matt\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}i:49;a:6:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n	\n\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";s:5:\"child\";a:2:{s:0:\"\";a:5:{s:5:\"title\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:77:\"WPTavern: Twitter’s New Official WordPress Plugin Prioritizes Extensibility\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"guid\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:28:\"http://wptavern.com/?p=39608\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:4:\"link\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:84:\"http://wptavern.com/twitters-new-official-wordpress-plugin-prioritizes-extensibility\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:11:\"description\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:6064:\"</span>
 **(unknown attachment: public.jpeg)** 
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">It’s easy to get overwhelmed when visiting the WordPress.org plugin directory in search of a Twitter plugin, as you’ll turn up</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/search.php?q=twitter%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>more than 3,000 Twitter-related extensions</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. Unless you’re running on recommendations or personal experience, it may not be easy to narrow down a quality plugin that keeps pace with changes to the Twitter API.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">This week Twitter finally decided to</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://dev.twitter.com/web/wordpress%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>release an official WordPress plugin</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">to assist users in optimizing their sites for a Twitter audience. I spoke with</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://twitter.com/niall%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Niall Kennedy</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, Developer Advocate at Twitter and contributor on its official WordPress plugin, about Twitter’s plans for the plugin. Kennedy is also listed as a contributor on both the official</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/facebook/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Facebook</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">and</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/vine/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>Vine</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">plugins for WordPress.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“Twitter regularly works with partners to implement Twitter features on their site,” Kennedy said.</span> <span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;"><b>“Shipping an official plugin allows us to directly support publishers powered by WordPress and provide an example implementation of Twitter best practices for publishers on any platform.”</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The plugin requires PHP 5.4 or later and offers many features that you many have already seen available via other extensions, including customization of embedded tweets, embedded Twitter videos, Tweet button for sharing content, Twitter Cards, integration with Twitter Analytics, a Follow button, and ads conversion tracking.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Many are wondering why anyone needs an official Twitter plugin, given that WordPress already has excellent oEmbed support for tweets.  Kennedy confirmed that the new plugin plays nicely with the built-in oEmbed support, while building on top of it:</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The Twitter plugin for WordPress enhances the oEmbed support built-in to WordPress with additional customization and functionality. We enhance the typical oEmbed response with additional integrations with WordPress APIs.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Our plugin loads Twitter’s widgets JavaScript asynchronously through wp_enqueue_script, omitting the script element typically inserted onto the page markup through the default oEmbed response.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Each embedded Tweet, added through a URL or shortcode, passes through the shortcode’s filter (shortcode_atts_tweet) for customization of embedded Tweet features across the site. The plugin handles fetching the correct oEmbed markup from Twitter’s servers and caching the unique result for the customization.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The plugin does not change MCE oEmbed handling in the admin view.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Kennedy declined to comment on why it took Twitter so long to release an official plugin, but hopes that WordPress developers will use it as a building block. Those who previously created Twitter plugins now have a solid example for how best to extend WordPress to customize Twitter’s latest features.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier-Bold;font-size:12pt;"><b>Designed to be Extensibile</b></span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">The development team behind the plugin plans to add new features to it as they become available from Twitter. They recognize that it will not be realistic to offer every imaginable feature, but instead are prioritizing extensibility to make sure developers can easily create their own customizations.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“The plugin includes</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://dev.twitter.com/web/embedded-video%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>support for displaying Twitter-hosted video embeds</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">, a new feature available to Twitter developers just in the past few weeks,” Kennedy said. “An official Twitter plugin makes it easy to roll out new features to publishers.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“We’re working with other plugin developers to integrate features through filters where any overlap may occur,” he said. Kennedy has a few ideas for the kinds of integrations that developers might want to build for the plugin using the WordPress APIs.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">“Our plugin automatically generates Twitter Card markup to represent a post, making some best guesses for post formats built-in to Core,” he said. “We expect plugin developers creating more specific display experiences will be able to build on top of our base features to select the best Twitter Cards template for their content.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Kennedy also believes the plugin can be further extended by SEO plugins to optimize content. “Some of the most popular WordPress plugins involve SEO,” he said. “We would like to integrate with SEO plugins to highlight the best titles, descriptions, and other detailed data provided by publishers to help their content stand out on Twitter.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">Additionally, he sees an opportunity for theme developers to make tweets a seamless part of a theme’s overall design. “Our embedded Tweet theming can be customized by theme authors to match the visual theme of their site,” Kennedy said. “Automatically adjusting Twitter theming components to a WordPress theme context would be pretty cool.”</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\n</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">If you’re looking to add the most common Twitter features to your WordPress site, you’ll be hard pressed to find a better plugin than the</span> <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/%22https://wordpress.org/plugins/twitter/%5C%22" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>official one on WordPress.org</u></a><span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">. Kennedy confirmed that Twitter plans to support the plugin through its official support forums on WordPress.org and will also have an escalated support channel for managed hosting providers.</span>
<span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}s:7:\"pubDate\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:31:\"Thu, 26 Feb 2015 20:10:48 +0000\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}s:32:\"http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/\";a:1:{s:7:\"creator\";a:1:{i:0;a:5:{s:4:\"data\";s:13:\"Sarah Gooding\";s:7:\"attribs\";a:0:{}s:8:\"xml_base\";s:0:\"\";s:17:\"xml_base_explicit\";b:0;s:8:\"xml_lang\";s:0:\"\";}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}s:4:\"type\";i:128;s:7:\"headers\";a:10:{s:6:\"server\";s:5:\"nginx\";s:4:\"date\";s:29:\"Tue, 10 Mar 2015 23:42:07 GMT\";s:12:\"content-type\";s:8:\"text/xml\";s:14:\"content-length\";s:6:\"214561\";s:10:\"connection\";s:5:\"close\";s:4:\"vary\";s:15:\"Accept-Encoding\";s:13:\"last-modified\";s:29:\"Tue, 10 Mar 2015 23:30:14 GMT\";s:15:\"x-frame-options\";s:10:\"SAMEORIGIN\";s:4:\"x-nc\";s:11:\"HIT lax 250\";s:13:\"accept-ranges\";s:5:\"bytes\";}s:5:\"build\";s:14:\"20130911040210\";}',	'no'),</span>

- <a href="applewebdata://53B4E767-2D66-426A-B2AA-63A3781179F0/'https://wordpress.org/news/2015/02/wordpress-4-1-1/%5C'" rel="noopener" class="external-link" target="_blank" style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;color:#e4afaff;"><u>WordPress 4.1.1 Maintenance Release</u></a> <span style="font-family:Courier;font-size:10.285714149475098pt;">February 18, 2015WordPress 4.1.1 is now available. This maintenance release fixes 21 bugs in version 4.1. Some of you may have been waiting to update to the latest version until now, but there just wasn’t much to address. WordPress 4.1 was a smooth-sailing release and has seen more than 14 million downloads in the last two months. For a full […]</span>
